objective: Test whether causal temporal context and calibrated forward-return distributions repair the unstable one-hour point-forecast defect without hiding transition costs or forcing activity.

diagnosis: Persistent position accounting removed fictitious hourly close-and-reopen costs but did not establish alpha.
One-hour median forecasts had weak, unstable rank association and were correctly set to zero after nonpositive calibration relations.
Static multiclass, two-stage hurdle, meta-label, threshold, and local-language-model veto lanes have already failed after-cost stability gates.
The next experiment must change the forecast target and temporal representation rather than relax gates or retune the consumed 2025-H1 outcomes.

research_basis.finding_used: Sequence models can learn persistent position signals, and transaction-cost-aware turnover regularization is more aligned with trading than point-return MSE.
research_basis.limitation: The published cost range was approximately 2 to 3 bps and continuous futures are not evidence for this crypto taker-cost setting.

research_basis.finding_used: Multi-task horizon and volatility supervision can improve a shared temporal representation.
research_basis.limitation: The reported 2000 to 2020 futures results and 3 bps costs are hypotheses only, not transferable performance evidence.

research_basis.finding_used: Intraday policies should preserve positional context because trading is sequential.
research_basis.limitation: This round does not adopt deep reinforcement learning or the paper's reported returns.

research_basis.finding_used: Crypto momentum must be evaluated with transaction costs, path risk, liquidation risk, and more than a mean-return test.
research_basis.limitation: No leverage or liquidation inference is permitted before an unlevered edge exists.

research_basis.finding_used: Learned offline action values can be overestimated under policy and data-distribution shift.
research_basis.limitation: Hindsight dynamic-programming Q labels and fitted-Q iteration are rejected from this round.

rejected_architectures.reason: Its oracle continuation policy creates a target-policy mismatch and optimistic action values that are not available in live operation.
A small crypto panel can overfit path-level objectives, and continuous differentiable positions do not match the hard 24-hour holding and fail-closed execution contract.
The live AMD host failed nn.LSTM training because aten::_thnn_fused_lstm_cell is unsupported by the installed DirectML stack.

data_contract.funding: Only funding settled inside each future holding interval enters the return target.
data_contract.forward_return_definition: For each horizon, compound the contiguous one-hour long returns net of settled funding but before transition costs: 10000 * (product(1 + hourly_net_fraction) - 1).
data_contract.role_boundary_rule: A row is admitted only when its complete 24-hour target path ends strictly inside the same role.
data_contract.missing_or_nonfinite_policy: Reject the run; no interpolation, synthetic values, or row-local imputation is permitted.

chronological_roles.purpose: Architecture development only; every role has been consumed by prior research and cannot support promotion.

normalization_contract.method: per-feature finite mean and population standard deviation
normalization_contract.target_scale_method: per-horizon training median absolute deviation about the training median, multiplied by 1.4826, with a 1 bps floor

model_contract.quantile_parameterization: For each horizon, predict a median and four nonnegative softplus gaps so q10 <= q25 <= q50 <= q75 <= q90 by construction.
model_contract.loss: Equal-weight normalized pinball loss across four horizons and five quantiles.
model_contract.ensemble: Elementwise median of the three seed forecasts; monotonicity must remain exact.
model_contract.compute_backend: DirectML required for this host run; CPU remains a separately declared portability mode

baseline_contract.primary: Training-role unconditional empirical quantile for each horizon, applied unchanged to every later row.
baseline_contract.zero_return: A zero forecast at every quantile and horizon.
baseline_contract.tabular_comparator: OpenCL LightGBM quantile models may run only after the causal TCN forecast gate passes; this prevents unnecessary model proliferation.

forecast_gate.gate_failure_action: Continue only to explicitly labeled descriptive policy diagnostics; deny tabular expansion, AI action review, promotion, leverage, and untouched data.

descriptive_policy_contract.reason: Measure activity and after-cost failure modes without representing them as selected trades or promotion evidence.
descriptive_policy_contract.position_sleeves: Fixed one-third notional sleeve per symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.entry_rule: When flat, select the horizon and side with the largest positive lower-quartile return after a 6 bps entry and 6 bps terminal exit. Long uses q25; short uses negative q75.
descriptive_policy_contract.holding_rule: Hold the selected side for exactly the selected 1, 4, 12, or 24 hour horizon, then flatten. No overlapping position is permitted within a symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.stress_rule: Reprice the exact fixed base trade ledger; do not reselect horizons, sides, or timestamps under stress.

economic_gate.promotion_result: Still denied because the complete Round 44 evaluation is selection-contaminated development evidence.

fin_r1_host_result.failure: cooldown_reconnect_state_uncertain risk_score 0.500 was below the frozen 0.600 minimum
fin_r1_host_result.gpu_residency: Ollama reported 6.2 GB and 100% GPU on the AMD host

ai_contract.architecture_review_observation: All three local models violated the requested output schema or produced internally inconsistent rankings in a bounded A/B/C architecture critique. Their text is nonbinding.
ai_contract.future_ai_role: Asynchronous research and risk review only after a deterministic primary model passes; any uplift requires a separate paired fixed-period test.
